def: s.f. Boastfulness, boasting, boast, exultation (syn. umang̠: the noun argues a verbal form bariyānā, which I have not however met with).
s.f. = bariyā, q.v. s.m. A whetter (of swords, &c.), knife-grinder.
s.f. Exemption, immunity, irresponsibility, freedom (from), release, discharge, liberation, exoneration, &c. (see barī).
adj. & s.m. Issuing, coming forth; apparent, manifest; — who or what comes forth; (in Arith.) the sum total or gross amount.
adj. In place, properly placed, arranged; true, accurate, right; on the ground, prostrate, quiet.
s.m. Small door, postern; verandah, portico; sun-shade.
s.m. = bārjāt, q.v.; — free grant of a spot of ground made by a zamīndār to any one of his relations.
s.m. Forbidding, prohibiting, restraining, checking; shunning, avoiding, avoidance; abandoning, leaving, desertion; dereliction; exclusion, exception, omission
adj. Befitting, proper, right, exact; opportune, à propos; prompt, ready; impromptu.
s.m. An oppressive custom of forcing to purchase goods above the market-price.
s.m. A harmless kind of snake.
v.t. To forbid, &c. (see barjan).
adj. See s.v. bar, 'boon.'
s.f. Lightning (syn. bijlī); electricity (see barqī)
subs: s.m. lit, 'Lightning-thrower'; matchlock-man, musketeer; guard; peon of a police station or thānā, constable; messenger of court; bailiff; — barq-andāzī, s.f. Post or office of barqandāz
wety: Borrowed from Arabic بَرْق (barq).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Large, great, senior, &c. = baṛā, q.v.; — s.m. The big, or bigger, one; the elder; elder brother, elder son.
s.m. An inferior clan of Rājpūts.
s.m. See s.v. barq. s.f. Lightning (syn. bijlī); electricity (see barqī)
subs: s.m. lit, 'Lightning-thrower'; matchlock-man, musketeer; guard; peon of a police station or thānā, constable; messenger of court; bailiff; — barq-andāzī, s.f. Post or office of barqandāz

def: adj. Fixed, established, firm; continuing as heretofore; standing, existing, extant (syn. ba-ḥāl)
subs: v.t. To establish, confirm, ratify; to uphold, maintain
v.n. To continue, remain, abide, last, stand, persevere, go on as before; to exist, subsist, be extant.

def: s.m. Spear grass.
adj. Little, few, some
subs: s.m. A little, a few, somewhat.

def: s.m. = barsh, q.v. s.m. Rain, &c. = baras, q.v.
subs: (S. varsha+aśana), s.m. Subsistence for a year; annual salary
(or barash-) kāl, s.m. The rainy season ( = barsāt)
(or barash-) kālī, adj. Pertaining to the rains, of the rainy season; rainy.

def: s.f. = barshā, q.v. s.f. Rain; the rainy season ( = barsāt)
subs: barshā-r̤itu kāl, s.m. The rainy season.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit वर्षा (varṣā).

def: v.t. = barshānā; barsānā, q.q.v. v.t. = barsānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to rain; to shower down, pour down; to scatter, sprinkle; to winnow.
s.m. = barshāśan, q.v. s.m. See s.v. = barsh. s.m. Rain, &c. = baras, q.v.
subs: (S. varsha+aśana), s.m. Subsistence for a year; annual salary
(or barash-) kāl, s.m. The rainy season ( = barsāt)
(or barash-) kālī, adj. Pertaining to the rains, of the rainy season; rainy.

def: s.f. Rising up, breaking up or closing (of a court or office); recalling or removal from office, recall, dismissal
subs: v.t. To break up (a court, &c.), dismiss, permit to depart; to remove from office, deprive of office
v.n. To rise or break up (as an assembly); to be closed, dissolved; to be dismissed, deprived of office.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَرْخَاسْت (barxāst).

def: s.f. Rising or breaking up (of an assembly, &c.), dissolution; dismissal removal from office.
s.m. See s.v. barḵẖ. adj. Little, few, some
subs: s.m. A little, a few, somewhat.

def: adj. & adv. Contradictory, adverse; inconsistent, untrue; the contrary (of, -ke), opposed (to), against; on the contrary
s.m. Name of the father of Áṣaf, the minister of Solomon. (He is fabled to have been fairy-born, whence the use of his name in amulets or spells to avert evil).
= P برخورد bar-ḵẖẉurd, = P برخوردار bar-ḵẖẉurdār, see s.v. bar, 'breast'; 'fruit.'
= P برخور bar-ḵẖẉur, = P برخوردار bar-ḵẖẉurdār, see s.v. bar, 'breast'; 'fruit.'
= P برخور bar-ḵẖẉur, = P برخورد bar-ḵẖẉurd, see s.v. bar, 'breast'; 'fruit.'
adj. Pertaining or relating to lightning; electric (e.g. tār-ě-barqī, s.f. Electric wire, telegraph wire).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian برقی (barqī).

def: s.m. A large well without a cylinder of masonry.
v.t. To bring to pass, bring about, accomplish, effect, produce, get out, extract; to gain; to exhibit.
s.m. A kind of gimlet or borer worked with a string; auger, drill (also written barmhā, barmāh: for this and other examples of the breaking up of भ into ब or ब and ह see bahin and bainh).
s.m. Armour, mail.
s.m. The plant Trichosanthes incisa (which is used medicinally, and is supposed to have the power of improving the memory).
s.m. = birmā, q.v.
s.m. = barmā, q.v. s.m. Armour, mail.
adj. & adv. In place; fitting, fit, suitable; opportune, apropos; reasonable; in time; in the nick of time, opportunely, seasonably (syn. bar-mauqaʻ; bar-waqt).
adj. & adv. Open, public, conspicuous; openly, in open day, publicly, in the sight of all.
v.t. To bore with a barmā, to bore, drill, pierce; to thrust in, drive home.
= bar-maḥat, q.v.
s.m. = barmā, q.v. s.m. Armour, mail.
s.m. The country called Burmah; the province of Ava; the Burmese.
s.m.f. Having a bad or ugly face, ill-looking, ugly; foul, nasty, forbidding; — a forbidding countenance; wry face; ill-looking or ugly person; — a cat.

s.m. Colour, hue, tint; sort, kind; class, tribe, order, caste, sect (the Hindūs are divided into four principal barns, viz. 1˚ Brāhman; 2˚ Kshatriya; 3˚ Vaiśya; 4˚ Śudra); fame, celebrity; praise; description; beauty; quality, property; (in Gram.) letter of the alphabet; syllable; (in Math.), coefficient
subs: s.m. The particular duties that are religiously incumbent on the several casts of Hindūs

s.m. Mixture or confusions of castes or tribes by intermarriage; a person or tribe of mixed origin, or from parents of different castes; a man who does not scruple to eat with one of a different caste
s.f. Alphabet; the a, b, c book
adj. & s.m. Out of caste, casteless; — one who is put out of caste; outcast.

def: v.t. To forbid, prohibit, &c.; — v.n. To leave off; to separate.
v.t. To light, &c. = bālnā, q.v. v.a. To kindle, to light (a fire, candle, &c.); to make a fire; to burn ( = bārnā).
s.m. The tree Cratæva Roxburghii.
s.f. Name of a small river running past the north of Benares into the Ganges.
v.t. To marry, wed.
v.n. To burn, &c. = balnā, q.v. v.a. To kindle, to light (a fire, candle, &c.); to make a fire; to burn ( = bārnā).
adj. & s.m. Young; — young man, youth.
v.n. = baṛhnā, q.v.
v.n. To enter, penetrate, go through ( = baṭhnā, baiṭhnā, paiṭhnā).
s.f. Youth, season of youth.
s.m. Delineation, description, representation, explanation; relation, recital, narration, mention; statement, assertion, account; writing; praise, commendation, panegyric
subs: (-kā, or -ko), To describe, explain, represent, mention, &c.; to praise, extol, &c.

def: v.t. To describe, &c. = barnan karnā, q.v.s.v. barnan. s.m. Delineation, description, representation, explanation; relation, recital, narration, mention; statement, assertion, account; writing; praise, commendation, panegyric
subs: (-kā, or -ko), To describe, explain, represent, mention, &c.; to praise, extol, &c.

def: adj. Tinted, coloured
subs: s.f. Coloured soil of two kinds (the one tinged with yellow, and the other with yellow and white).

def: s.f. Advance made for cultivation or manufacture.

s.f. See s.v. barn. s.m. Colour, hue, tint; sort, kind; class, tribe, order, caste, sect (the Hindūs are divided into four principal barns, viz. 1˚ Brāhman; 2˚ Kshatriya; 3˚ Vaiśya; 4˚ Śudra); fame, celebrity; praise; description; beauty; quality, property; (in Gram.) letter of the alphabet; syllable; (in Math.), coefficient
subs: s.m. The particular duties that are religiously incumbent on the several casts of Hindūs

s.m. Mixture or confusions of castes or tribes by intermarriage; a person or tribe of mixed origin, or from parents of different castes; a man who does not scruple to eat with one of a different caste
s.f. Alphabet; the a, b, c book
adj. & s.m. Out of caste, casteless; — one who is put out of caste; outcast.

subs: s.m. The particular duties that are religiously incumbent on the several casts of Hindūs

s.f. Alphabet; the a, b, c book
adj. & s.m. Out of caste, casteless; — one who is put out of caste; outcast.

def: corr. of barābar, q.v. adv. = bār-bār, and bārambār, q.q.v. s.f. Muttering, murmuring, grumbling, chattering; talking (in sleep), raving (in delirium)
subs: v.n. To mutter, &c. = baṛbaṛānā, and baṛṛānā.

def: s.m. Branch of the Baniyan or Indian fig-tree (bar).
s.m. = baṛūṅkhā, q.v. s.m. A kind of sugar-cane with long joints.
s.f. = bursī, q.v. s.f. Earthen pot or bowl for fire; crucible (of a goldsmith, &c.; syn. gursī).
s.m. = bareṭhā, q.v. s.m. Washerman (syn. dhobī); fuller; land of the third quality; a plot of ground on which sugar-cane has been lately grown.
s.m. Vestibule, veranda, porch (syn. ḍeʼoṛhī).
s.m. (?), Name of a tribe of cowherds (ahīrs) in the Mainpuri district.
s.f. = baṛhtī, q.v. Increase, augmentation, enhancement, advancement, progress, promotion, elevation, exaltation, prosperity, aggrandizement; excess, overplus.
adj. On foot; erected, set up, established, &c.: bar-pā rahnā, v n. To continue firm or standing
subs: v.t. To set on foot; to start, begin; produce, cause, occasion, raise, pitch, establish; to excite
v.n. To be raised, be set on foot, pitched, established; to take rise, rise, spring.

def: s.m. = baṛapan and baṛāʼī, q.q.v. s.m. Largeness, greatness, bulk, &c. ( = baṛāʼī, q.v.); grandeur, dignity, exaltation, elevation.
s.m. = baṛapan and baṛāʼī, q.q.v. s.m. Largeness, greatness, bulk, &c. ( = baṛāʼī, q.v.); grandeur, dignity, exaltation, elevation.
s.f. Wasp.
s.m. Dry land. land (opp. to baḥr); desert
subs: s.m. Rock-partridge; rock-pigeon; the Indian grouse ( = bhar-tītar)
adv. By land and by sea

def: s.m. Rope, string ( = barārā; barhā); a kind of rope used in a village game on the fourteenth of the light half of Kwār.
s.m. = برا barrā, q.v. s.m. Rope, string ( = barārā; barhā); a kind of rope used in a village game on the fourteenth of the light half of Kwār.
s.f. Muttering in sleep, &c. = baṛbaṛ, and baṛbaṛāhat, q.q.v. s.f. Muttering, murmuring, grumbling, chattering; talking (in sleep), raving (in delirium)
subs: v.n. To mutter, &c. = baṛbaṛānā, and baṛṛānā.

def: s.f. Seed of safflower or Carthamus tinctorius.
adj. Flashing, shining, brilliant, resplendent; white, snow-white, clean.
wety: From Arabic براق (barrāq).

def: s.f. Brilliancy, splendour.
v.n. To talk in one's sleep, &c. = baṛṛānā and baṛbaṛānā, q.q.v.
v.n. To murmur, mutter; to talk (in sleep), cry out (in nightmare), rave (in delirium)
subs: v.n. To rave in misery or want; to die of want, to starve.

def: adj. Of, or belonging to, or relating to, the land (barr, — as opp. to the sea or baḥr); of or belonging to the desert; wild.
s.m. (?), A name given to the uplands on the right bank of the Jamna.
s.m. The Indian grouse (see s.v. barr; & cf. bhar-tītar). s.f. Wasp.
s.m. A year
subs: adv. All the year round ( = baras-bhar).

def: = barshā, barsāt, q.v. s.f. Rain; the rainy season ( = barsāt)
subs: barshā-r̤itu kāl, s.m. The rainy season.

def: s.m. barsodiyā; barsoliyā, q.q.v.
s.m. See s.v. bar, 'breast.'
s.m. Raining; rain
subs: adj. Raining; rainy; about to rain, threatening rain ( = barsāʼū, q.v.).

def: v.t. To cause to rain; to shower down, pour down; to scatter, sprinkle; to winnow.
v.t. = barsānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to rain; to shower down, pour down; to scatter, sprinkle; to winnow.
adv. On or at the head or end (of), at the point
subs: v.n. To be on the point of business; to be ready when occasion demands; to be au fait at; to hold a situation, be employed; to be in possession of a desired object.

def: s.f. Rainy season, the rains (the third season, of the six, from the fifteenth of Ásārh to the fifteenth of Bhādoṅ); rain (in general).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀸𑀲𑀸𑀭𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺 (vāsāratti), from Sanskrit वर्षारात्रि (varṣārātri, “the rainy season”). Cognate with Gujarati વરસાદ (varsād).

def: adj. Relating to rain, rainy; belonging to the rainy season; — s.f. What is sown or produced in the rains; clothing or covering for the rainy season, or against rain; water-proof; apron of a carriage; portico; — name of a disorder in horses, the farcy (which usually breaks out in the rainy season).

ety: barsā, rt. of barsānā+ū = S. uka
def: adj. Raining; pertaining to rain, pluvious, rainy; ready to rain, threatening rain.
adj. & s.f. Annual; per annum; — annual tax or rent; annual rate of interest, &c.; annuity.
s.m. Rain, &c. = baras, q.v.
subs: (S. varsha+aśana), s.m. Subsistence for a year; annual salary
(or barash-) kāl, s.m. The rainy season ( = barsāt)
(or barash-) kālī, adj. Pertaining to the rains, of the rainy season; rainy.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian برش (buriš).

def: s.f. Rain; the rainy season ( = barsāt)
subs: barshā-r̤itu kāl, s.m. The rainy season.

subs: (S. varsha+aśana), s.m. Subsistence for a year; annual salary

(or barash-) kālī, adj. Pertaining to the rains, of the rainy season; rainy.

def: s.f. = barsāt, q.v. s.f. Rainy season, the rains (the third season, of the six, from the fifteenth of Ásārh to the fifteenth of Bhādoṅ); rain (in general).
adj. = barsāʼū, q.v. adj. Raining; pertaining to rain, pluvious, rainy; ready to rain, threatening rain.
adj. & s.f. = barsauṛī, q.v. adj. & s.f. Annual; per annum; — annual tax or rent; annual rate of interest, &c.; annuity.
s.m. See s.v. barsh. s.m. Rain, &c. = baras, q.v.
subs: (S. varsha+aśana), s.m. Subsistence for a year; annual salary
(or barash-) kāl, s.m. The rainy season ( = barsāt)
(or barash-) kālī, adj. Pertaining to the rains, of the rainy season; rainy.

def: adj. = baraswāṅ, q.v. adj. (f. -wīṅ), Yearly, annual, anniversary
s.f. A ceremony in commemoration of a deceased relation performed at the close of the first year after decease.
wety: From بَرَس (baras, “year”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.m. A servant engaged by the year ( = barsoliyā, barsāliyā).
s.m. = barsodiyā, q.v. s.m. A servant engaged by the year ( = barsoliyā, barsāliyā).

ety: S. वृत्त, or v.n. of H. bartnā, q.v.
def: s.m. Use, practice (syn. istěʻmāl; cf. bartāʼo).
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit व्रत (vrata).

def: s.f. Conversation, &c. = bāt, q.v.
subs: s.f. The advantages of conversation; conversation, discourse; exhortation, preaching.

def: s.m. That which has been used.
adv. On the ground, by reason (of); according to.
s.m. Vesse domestic utensil, dish, plate, basin, &c.
ex: عورت نے برتن صاف کرنے شروع کیے۔
’aurat ne bartan sāf karne śurū kiye.
The lady began cleaning the dishes.
wety: From برتنا (baratnā, “to use, make use of”) + ان (an). Compare Punjabi ਵਰਤਣ (vartaṇ) / وَرْتَݨ (vartaṇ).

def: s.m. Clothes that have been used; old or second-hand clothing.
v.t. To make or cause to use, to use, apply; to deal out (to), distribute, apportion, dispense.
s.m. Use, application; practice, custom; employment; disbursement, apportionment, expenditure; usage, treatment, conduct
subs: (-se), To act or behave (towards); to treat, use, deal (by or with).

def: adj. High; higher, superior; excellent.
adv. Aside, apart, away; on one side, out of the question
subs: v.t. To put or set aside, put away, get rid of; to remove, dismiss (from office); to eject, turn out
v.n. To be shelved, put aside or away, removed, dismissed, &c.

def: s.f. Dismissal, discharge.
s.f. Eminence, superiority, excellence.
s.f. = baṛhtī, q.v. Increase, augmentation, enhancement, advancement, progress, promotion, elevation, exaltation, prosperity, aggrandizement; excess, overplus.
v.t. To use, employ, apply, make use of; to distribute, dispense; to spend, expend.
adj. Existing, present, being, actually taking place, passing, current; dwelling, remaining, abiding; — s.m. (in Gram.), The present tense (syn. ḥāl).
s.m. (?), Land sown with sugar-cane after a rice-crop.
s.m. = bālū, q.v. s.m. A kind of drug and perfume (commonly called elā-bāluk).
s.m. = bālak, 'child,' q.v. s.m. Child, boy, &c. = bāl, q.v.
subs: To be neglectful of (one's) children or family; to desert (one's) family.

def: s.f. = bārūd, q.v. s.f. Gunpowder
s.m. A high jungle grass or reed, the writing reed, a species of Saccharum (but by some classed under Arundo karka, and by others under Calamus scriptorius).
s.f. Sandy soil of inferior quality, a mixture of sand and clay.
s.f. Gunpowder
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَارُود (bārūd), from Arabic بَارُود (bārūd). Doublet of بَارُوت (bārūt).

def: adj. Fallen off, fallen away; dwindled away.
conj. Moreover; but (also written baluk: cf. baran).

ety: from bar, 'fruit': ūmand = mand = S. mant
def: adj. Fruitful, fertile; successful, prosperous, fortunate, happy.
s.m. The god of water; — the tree Cratœva Roxburghii (also written baran, barnā).
s.f. Spirituous liquors, spirits.

ety: S. वरुणी, or वर्तनि, rt. वृ
def: s.f. Eyelash.
s.m. A kind of sugar-cane with long joints.
= bārūd, q.v. s.f. Gunpowder
s.f. Gunpowder
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَارُوت (bārūt), from Ottoman Turkish باروت (bârut), from Arabic بَارُود (bārūd). Doublet of بَارُود (bārūd).

def: s.f. Whiskers, moustaches.
s.m. A wooden ledge or fender round a carriage to prevent collision; armour, coat of mail; house, dwelling; host, swarm, multitude, heap.
s.f. Name of a rāginī or mode in music by which deer and serpents are said to be tamed; a kind of Hindī verse.
s.m. = barwā, 'the bird,' q.v. s.f. A certain bird that feeds on fish.
adj. & s.m. (prov.) = baṛā; baṛkā, q.q.v. s.m. Perquisite of the ahīr or cowherd in milk (generally the milk of every eighth day).

s.f. Mare; the nymph Aśvinī (who, in the form of a mare as the wife of Vivaswat or the Sun, became the mother of the two Aśvins); the personification of the constellation represented by a horse's head
subs: (vaḍavā+agni), s.m. Submarine fire, or the fire of the lower regions, fabled to be at the South-pole and not extinguishable by the sea-water
(vaḍawā+anala), s.m. = baṛwāgni; — (in Myth.), a being consisting of flame, but with the head of a mare, who sprang from the thighs of Urwā, and was received by the Ocean
s.m. 'Mare's mouth'; entrance to the lower or infernal regions at the South-pole; submarine fire ( = baṛwāgni).

def: s.f. A certain bird that feeds on fish.
s.m. Name of a class of hereditary watchmen near the Siwālik hills.
adv. In time; at the time (of); seasonably, opportunely, &c. ( = bar-maḥal, q.v.).
s.m. Name of a class of people whose occupation it is to cleanse and sell rice.
s.f. A kind of snake ( = barbat).
s.m. Tumour in the belly.
s.f. A kind of metre or verse in Hindī (cf. barwā).
adj. See s.v. bār, 'time,' &c.
s.m. = baryār, q.v. adj. & s.m. Strong, vigorous; violent; rich, fertile (as soil); — the plant Sida rhomboides or rhombifolia, and S. cordifolia (syn. balā).
adj. & s.m. Strong, vigorous; violent; rich, fertile (as soil); — the plant Sida rhomboides or rhombifolia, and S. cordifolia (syn. balā).
adj. & s.m. Strong, vigorous; violent; rich, fertile (as soil); — the plant Sida rhomboides or rhombifolia, and S. cordifolia (syn. balā).
Dialect. for baṛoṅ, q.v.s.v. baṛa.
s.m. A sown field; agriculture.

s.f.m. (?), A thing that intervenes between two things; a thing that makes a separation between two things or classes of things; interval, bar, partition; connecting link; the interval between death and the resurrection; — a picture of the imagination; a whimsical idea, fashion, or mode; a strange or quaint appearance (syn. ćeshṭā).
s.m. Mansion, dwelling; street (especially a long one); quarter of a town; desert, plain.
s.f. Perfume, fragrance, scent, smell, odour; offensive smell, stench; trace, sign, particle, soupçon; (syn. bo, bū)
subs: s.m. A vendor of perfumes, perfumer
s.f. lit. 'Possessing perfume'; a fragrant kind of rice, and of millet.

ety: old P. vaś; Zend vaśaṅh; S. vaśas
def: adj. Enough, sufficient, plenty; very much, too much, a great many; very; — adv. And so; in short, in a word; — intj. Enough! that will do! hold! stay!
subs: intj. (emphat. and colloq.), Cease! have done! no more!
v.n. To stop, have done, cease, desist, to give up, lay or put aside, put an end to, close, finish; to seize; to be satisfied
adv. To such an extent that; — inasmuch as, whereas.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بس (bas).

def: s.m. Will, authority, power, influence, command, control, grasp, mastership, supremacy; advantage, opportunity; help, remedy ( = ćāra); — postp. Under the power or control (of, -ke), in subjection (to); — bas ānā, or bas-meṅ ānā (-ke) = bas paṛnā, or bas-meṅ paṛnā (-ke), To be obtained; to come or fall into the power (of), be brought into subjection, be subdued; to be charmed, fascinated, bewitched
subs: (-se), To avail against, have power (over), be able to help oneself (against)

or bas-meṅ karnā or kar-lenā, To bring (one, -ko) under subjection (to oneself, apne), bring under control, get into (one's) power; to subdue, overpower, get the better of; to charm, fascinate, bewitch
adv. In the power (of), under the authority or control (of), in subjection (to); at the discretion (of)
bas-meṅ paṛnā, v.n. = bas ānā, q.v.
(apne), To have (one, -ko) under command or control; to manage, keep under, restrain, hold back
(-ke), To be subject (to), to submit (to), obey
adj. & s.m. Acting obediently to the will (of another); obsequious; obedient or docile person; obsequious person.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بس (bas).

def: s.m. Excitement, incentive, motive, cause; — raising (the dead) to life; resurrection
wety: Borrowed from Arabic بَعْث (baʕṯ).

ety: S. वास and वासकः, rt. वस्
def: s.m. Dwelling, lodging, abode, residence, shelter; nest (of a bird)
subs: bāsā karnā, v.n. To lodge, stay (with); to abide, dwell, reside; to make (its) nest, to roost (a bird).

def: s.m. lit. 'Yielding perfume'; the plant Justicia ganderussa ( = bākas, q.v.).
s.f. Marrow, fat, suet, adeps, brain; oily exudation.

ety: S. वास and वासकः, rt. वस्
def: s.m. Dwelling, lodging, abode, residence, shelter; nest (of a bird)
subs: bāsā karnā, v.n. To lodge, stay (with); to abide, dwell, reside; to make (its) nest, to roost (a bird).

def: adj. Much, many; very
adv. By reason (of), on account (of), through.
With or by a hundred (used only in Persian phrases; e.g. ba-ṣad měḥnat, with a hundred toils or exertions).
s.m. Bull, ox, bullock.
adv. With facility, easily.
s.m. See vāsak, bāsā, and bākas.
s.f. Millet; the heel; iron helmet.
s.f. The great vein in the arm, Vena basilica.
s.m. Vessel, plate, dish, basin, pot, &c.
wety: First attested in c. 1700 as Middle Hindi باسن (bāsan), inherited from Sanskrit वासन (vāsana). Cognate with Gujarati વાસણ (vāsaṇ) and Bengali বাসন (baśon).

def: adj. In the likeness; like, resembling.

ety: S. वस्न; and वसन; rt. वस्
def: s.m. Abiding, dwelling; residence, abode, habitation; price, wages; wealth; thing, substance
s.m. Covering, wrapper; cloth; clothes, dress, apparel, suit of clothes
s.m.f. (?), Perfume, odour, &c. = bās (syn. su-gandh).
wety: First attested in c. 1700 as Middle Hindi باسن (bāsan), inherited from Sanskrit वासन (vāsana). Cognate with Gujarati વાસણ (vāsaṇ) and Bengali বাসন (baśon).

def: v.t. To perfume (particularly by placing a thing in the midst of perfume); — v.n. To give forth a smell; to smell offensive
v.t. To cause to dwell; to settle a country; to bring into cultivation; to people, colonize, found a colony.
s.m. Epithet of Agni or fire; god of fire; sacred fire.

s.m. Spring, the vernal season (comprising Ćait and Baisākh, or from the middle of March to that of May); the vernal equinox; — name of one of the rāgs or musical modes of the Hindūs; — small-pox; — garland or wreath of yellow flowers
subs: v.n. To put forth yellow blossoms (like those of the mustard plant); to have yellow pustules, &c. on the face; to turn yellow or pale (generally with the words muṅh-par; e.g. uske munh-par basant phūlā); yellow sparks to fly (out of the eyes; e.g. āṅkhoṅ-meṅ basant phūlā; hence), to be dazzled, be astonished or amazed
s.f. The vernal festival of the Hindūs on the fifth day of the light half of the month Māgh
basant-rut, s.m. The season of spring
s.f. Knowledge of, or concern about, futurity.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit वसन्त (vasantá). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi بسنت (bsnt /⁠basant⁠/). Doublet of بَہار (bahār) and وَسَنْت (vasant).

def: adj. Of yellow colour, yellow (the colour of spring blossoms, such as those of the mustard-plant); — s.f. Yellow garment; — name of a goddess of small-pox.
s.m. Abiding, dwelling, living, life; residence, abode; homestead.
s.f. = basāʼo, and basgat, q.q.v. s.m. Abiding, dwelling, living, life; residence, abode; homestead.
s.m. A day; see vāsar.
adv. To a head; to the fore; to an end, at an end
subs: v.n. To come to an end, be finished, or accomplished; to succeed; to get away from; to come to the fore, come forward, contend or vie (with)
v.n. To reach the end, to be finished; to pass away
v.t. To bring to an end, finish, accomplish, execute; to spend, pass (time, &c.)
v.t. To bring to an end, &c. ( = ba-sar karnā); to acquit oneself of; to surpass, outstrip
v.n. To come to an end, be finished, spent, or passed; to vie (with), rival.

def: s.m. = baṣārat, q.v.; the eye ( = bāṣira, q.v.). s.f. corr. of A. bashārat, q.v. the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'

ety: for A. بصارة, inf. n. of بصر 'to see'
def: s.f. Seeing, perceiving, discerning; sense of sight, vision; insight, understanding, knowledge (cf. baṣīrat).

s.f. corr. of A. bashārat, q.v. the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'
part. adj. Inhabited; cultivated.
adj. Fetid, stinking ( = bisāhand); ill-flavoured, bitter; unwholesome, disagreeable; sour, morose.
adj. Sixty-two.
s.f. Bamboo-plantation ( = baswārī, q.v.). s.f. Bamboo-plantation; bamboo-thicket ( = basaur).
adj. = vāsava, q.v.
s.f. Ground-rent; rent paid by villagers who do not occupy any of the village lands.
s.m. Abode, dwelling, residence ( = basobās).
s.m. Mace, envelope of the nutmeg.
adj. & adv. Much, many, enough; in excess, abundantly.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بس (bas).

def: s.m. (?), An edible root which is found in lakes.
s.m. (local) A member of the sweeper caste in a village community, a sweeper ( = basoṛ).
adj. (f. -ī) = bisāhandā, q.v. adj. Having a fishy smell;: melling offensive or rank, stinking, rank, fetid; stale, dry, vapid, tasteless.
s.m. Night's lodging; roosting in its nest (a bird); a roost; roosting time; place of staying or halting
subs: (-ko), To give a night's lodging (to); to stop, halt (a bier, &c.); — to take a caged bird in the morning or evening to hear the singing of wild birds in a grove, with a view to its catching their notes
baserā lenā (-par), To roost; to repose

r: basfātaj, basfātij, bisfātaj, basfāyaj, bisfāyaj, basfāʼij, bas-pāya
def: s.m. The medicinal root polypody.

r: basfāyaj, bisfāyaj, basfāʼij, basfātaj, basfātij, bisfātaj, bas-pāya
def: s.m. The medicinal root polypody.
s.f. Residence, homestead; site of a village residence.
adj. Being, living, staying (used as last member of compounds, e.g. shab-bāsh, 'staying for the night; cf. bāsī, bāshī); — intj. Stop! stay! hold! be patient!
s.m. = bāsha, q.v.
s.m. A kind of falcon; hawk; sparrow-hawk.

ety: third pers. sing. of the aorist of būdan, 'to be'
def: adv. It may be; perhaps ( = shāyad, q.v.). adv. It may be, perhaps, perchance, possibly, probably
subs: It may be that it is, it may be so, perhaps it is.
wety: Borrowed from Persian باشد (bâšad).

ety: act. part. of būdan, or bāsh dan, 'to remain'
def: s.m. (pl. bāshindagān), Dweller, resident, inhabitant, denizen ( = bāsī, q.v.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian باشنده (bāšanda).

def: s.m. Man, human being; mankind, mortals.
s.m. Outer skin or cuticle; physiognomy, countenance, face, appearance; descriptive roll.

s.m. = P بشارت bashārat, bishārat, s.f. (for A. بشارة), Good news, glad tidings, joyful annunciation made to one in sleep or in a vision by some saint; happy dream or vision; divine inspiration, revelation.

s.f. = P بشاره bashāra, bishāra, s.m. (for A. بشارة), Good news, glad tidings, joyful annunciation made to one in sleep or in a vision by some saint; happy dream or vision; divine inspiration, revelation.
adj. Relating to man, human.
s.f. Humanity, human nature ( = insanīyat, q.v.). s.f. Human nature, humanity; human kindness, affability, politeness, urbanity. (See ādmīyat.)
adv. On condition, conditionally
subs: On the condition that, on the agreement or stipulation that, provided, so that.

def: s.f. Liveliness, cheerfulness, alacrity.
adv. Extremely, excessively; violently, forcibly.
fem. of bāsha, q.v.
s.m. Messenger of glad tidings (bashārat).
wety: From Arabic بَشِير (bašīr).

def: adj. Brisk, lively, cheerful, sprightly, in good spirits, pleased, delighted (syn. ḵẖẉush).
adj. With the inclusion (of, -ke), inclusive (of), including.
adj. Smelling, perfumed, fusty; stale; belonging to the day before
subs: s.f. The day after the fair
v.t. To make stale; to vomit
s.m. Unwashed face or mouth; empty stomach; — adv. With unwashed mouth; fasting
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi باسی (basy) (c. 1672), borrowed from Sanskrit वासिन् (vāsin).

def: adj. & s.m. Dwelling, inhabiting; — dweller, inhabitant (chiefly used as last member of compounds, e.g. ban-bāsī or vana-vāsī, 'inhabitant of the woods'; braj-bāsī, 'inhabitant of Braj or Brij)
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi باسی (basy) (c. 1672), borrowed from Sanskrit वासिन् (vāsin).

def: adv. In the form or mode (of); in the degree.
part. adj. Peopled, inhabited.
adj. Seeing; endowed with mental perception, knowing; (met.) weak-sighted, blind.
s.m. Sense or faculty of seeing; sight.
s.f. Sight, insight, mental perception; perceptive faculty of the mind; knowledge, understanding, intelligence, discernment; skill; circumspection, prudence.
s.m. Head man of a village (prop. basīṭh, q.v.).
adj. Simple, uncompounded, elementary; — s.m. Expanse, surface, superficies (syn. sat̤ḥ).

s.m. Chief person, or head manager, in a village (syn. muqaddam); one who is carefully observant of injunctions, or of all commanded or established rites and usages; (hence) agent, ambassador, envoy, messenger; go-between, intermeddler, busy-body, tale-bearer, mischief-maker; customer (in the language of harlots); — yoke attached to a plough (syn. jūhaṛ).

s.m. Chief person, or head manager, in a village (syn. muqaddam); one who is carefully observant of injunctions, or of all commanded or established rites and usages; (hence) agent, ambassador, envoy, messenger; go-between, intermeddler, busy-body, tale-bearer, mischief-maker; customer (in the language of harlots); — yoke attached to a plough (syn. jūhaṛ).
s.f. Business of a basīṭh; agency, ambassadorship, &c.
s.m. Name of a tribe of ahīrs or cowherds.
s.m. = basma, q.v.
s.m. Painting, staining, dyeing; dye, collyrium, tincture (see wasma).
s.f. See s.v. bās, 'perfume.' s.f. Perfume, fragrance, scent, smell, odour; offensive smell, stench; trace, sign, particle, soupçon; (syn. bo, bū)
subs: s.m. A vendor of perfumes, perfumer
s.f. lit. 'Possessing perfume'; a fragrant kind of rice, and of millet.

ety: bās˚ = Prk. वासे(इ) or वास(इ) = S. वासय(ति), rt. वास्
def: v.t. To perfume, scent.
s.f. Inclination, desire, expectation, trust, confidence; fancy, imagination.
s.m. Covering, cloth, wrapper, &c. ( = basan, q.v.; — syn. beṭhan; lapeṭan); bank, banking-firm.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀲𑀇 (vasaï), from Sanskrit वस॑ति (vásati), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hwásati, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂wéseti, from *h₂wes- + *-eti.

ety: bas = Prk. वस(इ) = S. वस(ति), rt. वस्
def: v.n. To dwell, abide; to be peopled, be settled, be populated, be cultivated; to be full, be well-peopled; to settle, encamp, lodge, perch, roost; to prosper, flourish, thrive.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀲𑀇 (vasaï), from Sanskrit वस॑ति (vásati), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hwásati, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂wéseti, from *h₂wes- + *-eti.

def: v.n. To be perfumed, be scented, to smell.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀲𑀇 (vasaï), from Sanskrit वस॑ति (vásati), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hwásati, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂wéseti, from *h₂wes- + *-eti.

def: s.m.f. (?), Perfume, odour, &c. = bās (syn. su-gandh).
s.f. Purse (syn. khuttī, thailī).
s.m. Abode, residence, &c. = bas-bās, q.v. s.m. Abode, dwelling, residence ( = basobās).
s.m. A caste of men whose occupation it is to make baskets of split bamboos; a member of that caste, a basket-maker; a member of the sweeper caste in a village community.
(for A. بصرة), prop. n. The city commonly called Bassorah, on the Persian Gulf.
s.m. Spreading or extending out, distension, diffusiveness.
part. Bound; shut; frozen
subs: v.t. To bind or collect together
v.n. To be bound or collected.

def: s.m. = بسته basta, q.v.

ety: Pehl. baśtak; Zend basta, rt. band; S. baddha, rt. bandh
def: part. adj. & s.m. Bound, shut, closed, fastened, folded up; frozen, congealed; — cloth in which anything is folded up, wrapper; parcel, bundle (as of papers or books), bale
subs: basta bāṅdhnā, v.n. To tie up a bundle, fold up and put away (papers, &c. in an office), to close (office).
wety: From Classical Persian بسته (basta, “sealed, closed, shut, package”).

def: adj. Populated, inhabited (a village).
s.f. Binding, contraction, stoppage, obstruction, constipation, concretion, congelation.
s.m. Servant, attendant, minister.
s.m. Servant, attendant, minister.
adj. Old, ancient, past; (in Southern India) after, afterwards ( = baʻd az ān).
s.f. Cloth for binding together bundles of papers, books, inkstands, &c.; wrapper.
s.m. The shrub Callicarpa lanata, or C. cana, or C. tomentosa (the root of which is employed in cutaneous diseases. It is also one of the trees used for making charcoal).
s.f. Inhabited place, settlement, colony; village, small town; abode, home; population, inhabitants (syn. ābādī).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi بستی (bsty) (c. 1657), semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit वसति (vasati).

def: s.m. Cloth, clothes, dress, garment, vesture, raiment, apparel.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بِسْتَر (bistar).

def: s.m. Site or foundation of a house, &c. (see vāstu).
s.f. Thing, matter, article, commodity, substance, wealth, property, means; things, goods, chattels, baggage (syn. asbāb)
subs: bast-ke gun, s.m. General properties of matter

def: s.m. Name of Krishṇa; see vāsudeva.
s.f. The world, the earth.
s.m. The chief of serpents, which is fabled to support the universe, and which was used as a string to whirl the mountain Mandar in churning the ocean for the amr̤it, &c.
s.m. The chief of serpents, which is fabled to support the universe, and which was used as a string to whirl the mountain Mandar in churning the ocean for the amr̤it, &c.

ety: S. वासि+dim. aff. ūlā, or olā = ū+S. लः
def: s.m. A kind of axe used by carpenters, an adze (dialectic forms are bāsilā, basilā, basulā).
s.f. A mason's or builder's hatchet, an instrument for cutting bricks or stone.
adv. In the appearance (of), in the form or guise (of); in the manner (of), by way (of); in appearance, apparently, outwardly.
s.f. = bāṅsrī, q.v. s.f. Pipe, fife, flute, flageolet
subs: v.n. To pipe, to play on the flute.

def: s.f. = beswā, q.v. s.f. Prostitute, harlot, courtesan; crafty or cunning woman.
s.f. Bamboo-plantation; bamboo-thicket ( = basaur).
adj. & s.m. See s.v. bas. s.f. Perfume, fragrance, scent, smell, odour; offensive smell, stench; trace, sign, particle, soupçon; (syn. bo, bū)
subs: s.m. A vendor of perfumes, perfumer
s.f. lit. 'Possessing perfume'; a fragrant kind of rice, and of millet.

def: s.f. Speech, language, word, saying, conversation, talk, gossip, report, discourse, news, tale, story, account; thing, affair, matter, business, concern, fact, case, circumstance, occurrence, object, particular, article, proposal, aim, cause, question, subject
subs: adv. In every word, in every case or instance, on every occasion, for the least thing; in every particular, in every respect, throughout, altogether
To depart from (one's) word, to say one thing and do another; to equivocate, prevaricate, shuffle
To prolong a contest or dispute, to spin out or continue an altercation; to make a serious affair of
To mar a plot, to spoil; to thwart, frustrate; to ruin one's credit, bring disgrace upon
To lose credit, become bankrupt, be ruined
To talk much; to make up a story; to invent excuses, to concoct, fabricate; to talk grandly, to boast, bounce
bāt-banāʼū, s.m. One who makes excuses; time-server
To be snccessful, prove a success, answer well; to gain credit or honour, to prosper, flourish
To attain (one's) object or end, effect (one's) purpose; to get to the bottom of an affair, to fathom a design or object
To be bent or set upon, to insist; to hold or establish a position or point, &c.
(-kī), To seize censoriously on what is said, to carp or cavil (at)
bāt phernā, To go back from one's word; to prevaricate, shuffle, &c. ( = bāt badalnā)
To put a question (to), inquire (of, -se); to inquire (after); to evince concern, care, or feeling, &c. (for)
To talk (at), to taunt, jeer, mock
To put off; to make excuses; to evade
To talk (of, -kī), to converse, broach conversation, start a subject
To talk much, &c. = bāt banānā, q.v.
To broach conversation, &c. ( = bāt ćalānā); to trake lup or renew a dispute or altercation
s.f. Talk, conversation, discourse; — bāt-ćīt karnā, To talk, converse, &c.; — bāt dohrānā, To repeat a thing, to harp on a thing
To persist in making excuses, to put off, evade, shuffle
To throw away one's words, to ask in vain; to ask for, request; to propose
To agree, assent, comply; to keep one's credit; to assist
To have (one's) words made good; to be fulfilled or accomplished (declarations); to succeed; to overcome, get the better in argument, to prevail; to be accepted; to preserve perfect
To multiply words, talk much, make much of little, to exaggerate
bāt-kā pūrā, bāt-kā saććā, adj. True to one's word, faithful to one's promise, trustworthy; — bāt-kī bāt-meṅ, adv. In a word, in an instant, suddenly
To cut one's speech short, speak when another is speaking, to interrupt; to break the thread of a discourse
(One's speech) to be approved or accepted
To speak, talk, converse, &c. ( = bāteṅ karnā, bāt-ćīt karnā)
To swallow or gulp down one's words; to bear in silence, wait, have patience, suppress one's feelings
(kisī-ke) muṅh-se le-jānā, To take the words out of (one's) mouth; to anticipate one in speaking of or mentioning a thing
To leak out, become known or public, be discovered, to transpire
To speak (of), tell, say; to talk, discourse
bāt karte-meṅ, adv. In an instant, all at once
To frame (one's) speech or words carefully; to speak to the purpose, or with effect
To speak (of or about), make arrangements (for), negotiate; to impute (to), charge (with), to calumniate, traduce
To feel, or be impressed by, one's words; to be offended
To turn off, evade, or divert a discourse; to nullify a speech or saying; to silence, refute, rebut; to speak at, taunt; to snub, put down
bāt qabū, karnā, To heed or mind what is said, to obey; to admit, accept, agree to, comply with
To remain in the memory, be kept or borne in mind
adv. In a short time, in a little while, in a moment
bāt-meṅ bolnā, bāt-meṅ paṛnā (-kī), To put in a word whilst another is speaking, to interrupt (one) in speaking ( = bāt kāṭnā)
To drag out or prolong an argument or dispute; to carp or cavil at an argument, to convict or refute one from his own lips; to draw inferences from; to learn from
To lose credit or repute, &c. ( = bāt bigaṛnā, q.v.)

def: s.m. Pain in the joints, rheumatism.

def: s.m. The worm which is destructive to shipping, ship-worm, Teredo.

def: s.m. A line drawn to distinguish separate names in a catalogue, or separate items in an account; line, mark, score.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بت (bat, “mark, score”).

def: s.m. The banyan or Indian fig-tree, Ficus indica (commonly baṛ); a small shell, the Cyprœa moneta or cowry.

s.m. Division, partition, portion, share, lot ( = bāṅṭ, q.v.). s.m. = bāṭ, 'a weight,' &c. q.v. s.f. def: s.m. Turn, twist, fold, ply, plait, wrinkle or fold on the belly (from fatness, &c.); tripe.
